title: Polestar Just Had Its Best First Quarter Ever
text: Polestar achieved record retail sales volumes in Q1 2026 with 13,126 cars sold globally, representing 7% year-over-year growth. The milestone demonstrates the company's ability to scale production and sales operations despite significant macro headwinds. Retail sales points increased from 154 at the end of Q1 2025 to 230 by the end of Q1 2026, representing 50% growth in the retail network. This expansion supports the company's goal to reach approximately 250 sales points globally by the end of 2026. Sales growth was driven primarily by the Polestar 3 and 4 models, which continue to capture strong customer demand. Revenue growth of 7% in unit sales reflects steady progress on volume targets, though profitability remains pressured by tariffs and pricing competition. The company maintained a cash position of $676 million after securing $1.4 billion in renewed facilities. Management projects low double-digit volume growth for 2026 with continued retail network expansion. Future product launches, including the Polestar 5 and new Polestar 4 variants, are expected to drive further volume growth and margin improvement.
